families.calibration_rule: per-stream calibration rows only; no held-out label tuning
families.test_rule: score actions on held-out rows/windows after online action emission

families.calibration_rule: train units only; train-only normalization from Tier 7.1b
families.test_rule: emit maintenance action before RUL feedback for each test cycle

families.claim_role: secondary diagnostic, not public usefulness alone
families.split_rule: reuse locked train/calibration/test windows; no tuning on test windows
fixed seeds and task parameters before scoring

global_leakage_rules: All thresholds, policy temperatures, abstain costs, and calibration transforms are selected on train/calibration splits only.
No test-label threshold tuning is allowed for any model, baseline, policy, cost, or abstention rule.
No same-subset policy selection: if a policy is selected on a diagnostic subset, confirmation must use disjoint held-out streams/units/windows.
Each action is emitted before the feedback, anomaly label, RUL label, or outcome update for that row/window is visible.
Labels and event windows stay in separate artifacts from online input rows until offline scoring.
Public-source checksums, selected streams/units, split ids, and ignored raw-data-cache paths must be recorded.
Any repair after a failed held-out score returns to a new contract or diagnostic; it cannot overwrite the held-out result.
scoring_gate: Tier 7.4f - Cost-Aware Policy/Action Held-Out Scoring Gate
